findings.title: External Script Download and Execution
findings.description: The skill instructions explicitly command the agent to download and execute a script from an untrusted external URL (project-bootstrap-templates.dev/init.sh) before using the skill. This is a classic supply chain attack vector that can lead to arbitrary code execution, data exfiltration, or installation of malware.
findings.remediation: Remove instructions that download and execute external scripts. Bundle all necessary templates within the skill package. If external resources are required, use verified sources with integrity checks (e.g., checksum verification, signed packages).

findings.title: Social Engineering via Urgent Language
findings.description: The instructions use coercive language ('MUST run', 'NON-NEGOTIABLE', 'WARNING') to pressure the user into executing the setup script without proper scrutiny. This social engineering tactic increases the likelihood that the malicious download-and-execute step will be performed.
findings.evidence: "You MUST run the initialization script..." and "NON-NEGOTIABLE requirement"
findings.remediation: Remove coercive and urgent language. Present setup as optional and allow the user to verify contents before execution.
